args.query: site:slack.com/help billing invoices download workspace owner billing page Slack

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Manage your Slack plan and billing details

  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details

  - source: Slack Help Center

  - domain: slack.com


  ## Content


  # Manage your Slack plan and billing details


  Slack explains that plan and billing details for a workspace are handled from the
  Billing page. The people who can manage these billing details include Workspace
  Owners and the members who originally upgraded the workspace.


  The billing page is reached from Slack on desktop. Open the Admin area in the sidebar,
  then choose Manage billing. The Billing page contains sections for plan details,
  payment methods, billing history, billable members, billing statements, and billing
  contacts.


  ## Change your billing cycle


  To move between monthly and annual billing, open Admin from the desktop sidebar
  and choose Manage billing. From the Overview tab, choose the option to change plan
  details. In the billing-cycle section, choose monthly or annual billing, continue
  to checkout, review the order, and complete the purchase.


  ## Manage your payment method


  Payment methods are managed from the Billing Details tab. A workspace can add a
  new card, set a default payment method, switch to an eligible bank account payment
  method, or remove a saved card. These changes are billing-account changes and are
  handled inside Slack''s billing interface.


  ## Switch to invoicing


  Eligible annually billed workspaces can switch to self-serve invoices and pay by
  check or direct deposit. In Slack, open Admin, choose Manage billing, open the Billing
  Details tab, and choose the option to pay by invoice. Slack then asks whether the
  change should take effect immediately or at the next renewal date. The user continues
  to checkout and reviews the order to finish the change.


  ## View your billing history


  Slack''s instructions for billing history use the same official billing path. From
  desktop, open Admin in the sidebar, select Manage billing, and open the History
  tab. To view a billing statement, choose a month with the calendar control and go
  to that month. Billing statements can be viewed and downloaded from this official
  billing history area.


  ## View billable members


  Slack separates billing history from billable-member reporting. For Pro and Business+
  workspaces, the Workspace Primary Owner can create a billable members report from
  the Billing page. The report covers a chosen end date and the preceding twenty-seven
  days, and can also include billable monthly active members from linked workspaces.
  Enterprise organizations have a separate organization-level billing path for creating
  this type of report.


  ## Edit billing statements


  Company name and address information for future billing statements is managed by
  editing billing details. Open Admin, choose Manage billing, open the Billing Details
  tab, edit the company or address information, and save the billing address. Slack
  notes that these updates apply to future billing statements. Already issued billing
  statements for credit card payments cannot be edited after issuance.


  ## Add billing contacts


  A workspace can add additional billing contacts so accounting or finance contacts
  receive billing-related emails. Billing contacts do not manage billing details,
  but they may see workspace billing information, including billing statements and
  Workspace Owner contact information. To add one, open Admin, choose Manage billing,
  open the Contacts tab, add a new billing contact, enter the contact information,
  and save it.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Manage your Slack plan and billing details
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/218915087-Manage-your-Slack-plan-and-billing-details

Your workspace is billed **directly by Slack** on the **Business+** plan. However, your account is a **Workspace Admin** without billing-management permission, so you may not be able to access or download the statement yourself.

The correct path for an authorized billing administrator is:

1. Open Slack on desktop.
2. Select **Admin** in the sidebar.
3. Choose **Manage billing**.
4. Open the **History** tab.
5. Select the relevant month and view or download the billing statement.

Slack says billing access is generally available to the **Workspace Owner** or the member who originally upgraded the workspace. You should ask your Workspace Owner or billing administrator to download the recent statement, or have them add you as a billing contact if appropriate. Billing contacts can view billing information and statements, but do not manage billing settings.

I did not download or request the invoice because your account currently lacks billing-management permission.

final_answer:
Your workspace is billed **directly by Slack** on the **Business+** plan. However, your account is a **Workspace Admin** without billing-management permission, so you may not be able to access or download the statement yourself.

The correct path for an authorized billing administrator is:

1. Open Slack on desktop.
2. Select **Admin** in the sidebar.
3. Choose **Manage billing**.
4. Open the **History** tab.
5. Select the relevant month and view or download the billing statement.

Slack says billing access is generally available to the **Workspace Owner** or the member who originally upgraded the workspace. You should ask your Workspace Owner or billing administrator to download the recent statement, or have them add you as a billing contact if appropriate. Billing contacts can view billing information and statements, but do not manage billing settings.

I did not download or request the invoice because your account currently lacks billing-management permission.
